extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters filed by and against each category. I counted only the “Against” rows: criminal proceedings are Nil for the Company, Directors, Promoters, KMPs, SMPs and subsidiaries, while statutory/regulatory actions are Nil throughout; tax proceedings against the Company are 10, with promoter tax matters excluded as personal matters, and subsidiaries show Nil. The quantified aggregate amount against the Company is ₹425.55 million; no quantified amount is shown against subsidiaries, and all “By” rows are excluded, giving ₹42.555 crore after conversion. Contingent liabilities are disclosed as separate Fiscal 2025 line items without a stated Total row, so the contingent-liability components (₹300.95 million tax litigations + ₹4,131.45 million bank guarantees/performance, earnest money and security deposits + ₹142.55 million arbitration proceedings + ₹4.50 million motor vehicle accident litigations) were summed to ₹4,579.45 million, or ₹457.945 crore, as at March 31, 2025; commitments were excluded. The company-wide top-10 customer concentration is 64.12% for Fiscal 2025, calculated as a percentage of revenue from operations; no company-wide top-five percentage is disclosed. Litigation and contingent-liability tables use ₹ million, converted to crore by dividing by 10 (1 crore = ₹10 million).
